svg.text: W↔W centreline crossings — what we skip and why
Two web members' centrelines mathematically cross in elevation, but they don't physically touch in assembly (they're separate sticks stacked on chord webs).
FrameCAD doesn't fasten W↔W in Linear trusses. Our simplifier follows the same rule — skip these crossings, keep only web↔chord and chord↔chord.
GREEN = web↔chord or chord↔chord = WEB HOLE placed
RED X = web↔web — skipped (no physical contact)
Both types are mathematical centreline crossings.
Across these 4 frames: 144 web↔chord crossings kept · 0 W↔W crossings skipped (correctly — no physical fastener at those points)
